name: sanctuary-inner
summary: The innermost sacred space — priests only. Beyond the nave, behind a veil or screen, where the true relic or divine focus is kept. Charged, silent, heavy with presence.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: square or slightly long — a focal chamber, not a hall

accent.purpose: central dais or inlaid circle around the focus

primary_palette.note: high altar flanked by tall candelabra at each corner
central relic casket on a dais
colossal deity effigy as the focus of the chamber
an inlaid ritual circle bordered by four pillars

story_prompts: the high priest at vigil — alone, kneeling before the relic, mouth moving silently
the veil torn down — someone has violated the sanctuary, the focus is gone
between rites — perfectly arranged, every candle trimmed, no one present
a relic recently returned — wrapped cloths and travel-stains still on the casket
feast-day preparation — extra banners hung, fresh oil in every lamp

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: focused and reverent (0.25-0.35) — strong halo around the focus, corners in shadow

anti_patterns: strict bilateral symmetry — the sanctuary is a formal space
the focus sits at the exact geometric center or directly opposite the door
no pews — this is not a worship hall; only a priest or two is ever here
